results.title: Turn off the Eiffel Tower
results.content:
newsletter icon

July 19th, 2026

# Turn off the Eiffel Tower

## Turn off the Eiffel Tower

gm legends, happy Sunday.

Today: a world map with a filter that deletes famous places, open source comes for your $300 SEO bill, and a duck that knows that discount is fake.

## This map hides famous places

Alban (@flightmussy), whose whole Product Hunt bio is "creating stuff for fun," built Detourmap: one interactive map of 70,000 places pulled from Wikidata, from waterfalls and catacombs to 584 ghost towns, each ranked on a fame scale that runs from world-famous down to hidden gem. Free, no account, no ads. [...] 🔥 Our Take: Google Maps knows every one of these places and will never show them to you, because it ranks by popularity and popularity is a loop: famous places get the pins, the pins keep them famous. Detourmap's fame filter breaks the loop. Narrow it to hidden gems and the Eiffel Tower vanishes while a Soviet mining town on Svalbard stays. One commenter lost twenty minutes wandering Romanian castles. The whole dataset is downloadable and public domain. This is the good kind of internet.

## Ahrefs has an open source problem [...] 11

results.title: Beauty Diagram: Diagrams that don't look like they were auto-generated | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Liviu Chita

the Figma detour is the tax every Mermaid user knows too well. the CLI/API angle is the right call for making this actually stick in a workflow rather than being a one-off tool. curious about the GIF export specifically for animated playback: is the animation sequenced to follow the actual logic flow of the diagram, or is it more of a visual fade-in? that distinction matters a lot for whether it communicates the flow or just looks nice

Report

Levi.l

Beauty Diagram

Beauty Diagram

Maker

@liviu\_chita Sharp distinction — and honestly one we’ve thought a lot about. [...] Liviu Chita

@levi840714 the "nice by default, flow-aware as opt-in" framing is exactly the right call and honestly relieved to hear you're thinking about it that way. forcing semantic sequencing across every diagram type would create more problems than it solves since a swimlane and a flowchart have completely different ideas of what comes next. the opt-in approach also gives you a really natural moment where basic animation works out of the box and precise flow control becomes the thing teams actually want to pay for once they feel the difference. on the roadmap side, the batch mode with markdown extraction is the one i would push up the list. that workflow is where people will stop treating it as a one-off tool and start relying on it every day. genuinely rooting for this one 🙌 [...] That’s where I want to take it next: opt-in flow-aware animation per diagram type, and eventually more control over sequence/timing. The goal is: nice by default, with manual controls for timing, sequence, and effects when you need the animation to communicate a specific flow.

And yeah, glad the CLI/API direction lands.

Report

Liviu Chita

No reviews yetBe the first to leave a founder review for Review Widget

•1 review

I'm extremely disappointed with this review widget. The product's functionality is severely lacking since I couldn't even connect my account or test it. Despite multiple attempts to reach out, customer support remained unresponsive. This combination of a non-functional product and poor support forces me to look for a better alternative. I give it two stars instead of one, because I like the design and clean looks of the product.
